note_data.title: Atomic structure
keyPoints: Elements and atoms form the foundation of atomic structure
Inside the atom contains the basic components that make up atomic structure
Numbers of nucleons are fundamental to understanding atomic composition
Isotopes are variations of atoms with different nucleon numbers
The number of protons, neutrons and electrons determines atomic identity
Atomic structure is directly connected to electronic structure in atoms

definitions.definition: Referenced as a key component of atomic structure with varying numbers of nucleons
Particles that make up the atomic nucleus, with their numbers being significant to atomic identity

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Use the format 1s² 2s² 2p⁶ or [Ne] 3s² for shortened form
Use trends and give reasoning based on atomic structure

modelAnswer.question: Explain why the first ionisation energy of sodium is less than that of magnesium. [3]
modelAnswer.answer: Sodium has electronic configuration 1s² 2s² 2p⁶ 3s¹ while magnesium has 1s² 2s² 2p⁶ 3s². Magnesium has a higher nuclear charge (+12) compared to sodium (+11). The outer electron in magnesium experiences greater nuclear attraction, so more energy is required to remove it.
markBreakdown: Mark 2: Higher nuclear charge in magnesium identified
Mark 3: Link to greater nuclear attraction requiring more energy

commonMistakes: Confusion about the relationship between protons, neutrons and electrons
examinerTips: Focus on understanding the numbers of protons, neutrons and electrons
Remember that isotopes are key to atomic structure concepts
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Orbital shapes diagram showing spherical s-orbital and dumbbell-shaped p-orbitals in 3D, plus electronic configuration notation with arrows showing electron spin pairing
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q2 (3 marks) — they asked students to state the relative masses and charges of protons, neutrons and electrons in a table format
N/D 2022 P1 Q4 (2 marks) featured atomic structure calculation asking candidates to determine the number of neutrons in chlorine-37 given the atomic number
M/J 2022 P2 Q1 (4 marks) required students to draw and label the structure of a lithium atom showing electron arrangement in shells
N/D 2023 P1 Q6 (3 marks) tested isotope understanding by asking students to explain why carbon-12 and carbon-14 have the same chemical properties but different physical properties

workedExample.question: An atom of sulfur has atomic number 16 and mass number 32. (a) State the number of protons, neutrons and electrons in this atom. (b) Draw the electronic structure of sulfur showing electrons in shells. [5 marks]
steps: Step 1: Identify that atomic number = number of protons = 16
Step 2: Calculate neutrons using mass number - atomic number = 32 - 16 = 16 neutrons
Step 3: For neutral atom, electrons = protons = 16 electrons
Step 4: Arrange electrons in shells following 2,8,8 rule: first shell holds 2, second shell holds 8, third shell holds remaining 6
Step 5: Draw nucleus with 16p and 16n, then three concentric circles with 2, 8, and 6 electrons respectively
workedExample.answer: (a) Protons: 16, Neutrons: 16, Electrons: 16 (b) Electronic structure: 2,8,6 with appropriate diagram showing nucleus and electron shells
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark each for correct number of protons, neutrons, electrons; 1 mark for correct electron arrangement 2,8,6; 1 mark for clear diagram with labeled shells

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused mass number with atomic mass and gave decimal values' — always use whole numbers for mass number in atomic structure calculations
Examiner Report 2022: 'Students frequently forgot to show the nucleus when drawing atomic diagrams' — always include and label the nucleus with protons and neutrons when drawing atomic structure
Examiner Report 2023: 'Candidates often stated that isotopes have different numbers of protons' — remember isotopes have the same number of protons but different numbers of neutrons
note_data.keyDiagram: Students must be able to draw an atom showing: (1) Central nucleus containing protons (p) and neutrons (n) clearly labeled, (2) Concentric circles representing electron shells, (3) Electrons shown as dots or crosses on the shells, (4) Correct electron arrangement following the 2,8,8,2 pattern, (5) All particles labeled with their charges if required. The diagram should be proportional with nucleus much smaller than electron shells.
note_data.quickRecap: Atomic structure covers elements and atoms, focusing on what's inside the atom. The numbers of nucleons (protons and neutrons) are crucial, along with understanding isotopes and how to determine the numbers of protons, neutrons and electrons in different atoms.
